evals.name: macos-update-blocked-by-space
evals.prompt: ugh my macbook says i don't have enough space to install the macOS update, i think i need like 20gb free and i'm nowhere close. it's a 256gb machine. can you figure out what's eating everything and help me clear some out? i do a lot of dev work (react native / expo, some node stuff).
evals.expected_output: Runs the read-only scanner first, presents a ranked tiered plan of the biggest reclaimable items (Docker image, caches, simulators, etc.) with sizes and safe reclaim commands, totals up easy wins, and offers to clean tier-by-tier with confirmation rather than deleting blindly. Should surface that a chunk of used space is system-level (needs sudo).

evals.name: vague-disk-full-symptom
evals.prompt: my mac is basically full again, the storage bar in settings is all red. what's taking up all my space?
evals.expected_output: Triggers the skill from the symptom alone, runs the scanner, and reports what's actually consuming space (drilling into hidden parents like Containers/dotdirs, not just top-level du). Distinguishes purgeable space and explains the df-vs-actual gotcha if relevant. Does not start deleting without being asked.

evals.name: targeted-dev-cache-cleanup
evals.prompt: i want to clean up all the developer junk on my mac — old xcode derived data, simulators i don't use, npm/brew caches, that kind of thing. don't touch anything risky though, i've broken my setup before doing this.
evals.expected_output: Scans, then proposes only the safe regenerable dev caches (DerivedData, unavailable simulators via simctl, npm/brew cache clean) using app-native commands, explicitly keeping risky items (app data, archives that may be needed) out of scope. Confirms before deleting and honors the user's caution.
